name: VectorCode
what_it_does: VectorCode is a code repository indexing tool that uses tree-sitter semantic chunking and Chroma vector embeddings to inject task-relevant codebase context into LLM prompts, reducing hallucination on proprietary or niche codebases. It provides a CLI, Neovim Lua API, and MCP server.
use_cases: Indexing a private or niche codebase so LLMs have accurate context when answering questions about it
Building Neovim AI plugins that retrieve semantically relevant code chunks before querying an LLM
Providing MCP-connected agents with precise code search and retrieval over large repositories
not_for: Teams using IDEs other than Neovim (primary integration is Neovim; MCP is secondary)
Use cases requiring cloud-hosted vector search (Chroma runs locally)
Simple projects where the LLM's built-in context window is sufficient
best_when: You are a Neovim user with a large or non-public codebase where LLMs frequently hallucinate, and you want semantic code retrieval integrated into your editor's AI workflow.
avoid_when: You need a GUI-based or IDE-agnostic solution — the primary UX is Neovim-centric.

auth.notes: No authentication required. Local Chroma vector database.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed open source. Embedding model costs depend on provider chosen.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Beta software (v0.7.x) with active development; 734+ commits and 17 contributors indicate solid engineering.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Indexing operations are idempotent; search is read-only.
known_agent_gotchas: Minor version bumps indicate breaking changes — pin versions in agent configs
Local Chroma DB must be running for MCP tools to function
Initial indexing of large repos can be time-consuming
Neovim-first design means MCP interface may lag behind Lua API features
